changed.from: Fix c > 1, q_max in (0,1), and k = ceil(c beta). There exist positive multiplicative constants depending only on c and q_max such that, for every beta in N and q in (0,q_max], every schedule p in S_{k,q} satisfies A_beta(p) bounded below by a constant times (rho_Ch(c,q_max) / q)^(2 beta), while the shifted Chebyshev-Lobatto schedule p^Ch(k,q) satisfies A_beta(p^Ch(k,q)) bounded above by a constant times (rho_Ch(c,q_max) / q)^(2 beta). Hence M_{beta,k,q} has the same low-budget exponential base rho_Ch(c,q_max) / q, the order 2 beta is intrinsic on q in (0,q_max], and the equal-spacing beta / q factor from prop:equal-spacing-benchmark is not minimax once k > beta + 1.
changed.to: Fix c > 1, q_max in (0,1), k=ceil(c beta), and define the pointwise Chebyshev base rho(q)=(1+sqrt(1-q))^2. There exist positive constants C_-(q_max) and C_+(c,q_max) such that, for every beta>=1 and q in (0,q_max], every p in S_{k,q} satisfies A_beta(p) >= C_-(q_max) (rho(q)/q)^(2 beta), while the shifted Chebyshev-Lobatto schedule satisfies A_beta(p^Ch(k,q)) <= C_+(c,q_max) (rho(q)/q)^(2 beta). Hence M_{beta,k,q} has pointwise low-budget exponential base rho(q)/q; as q downarrow 0 this is 4/q, the intrinsic exponent is 2 beta, and the equal-spacing beta/q factor is not minimax in beta once the rollout uses more than beta+1 nodes.
changed.reason: The frozen cap-level definition rho_Ch(c,q_max)=sup-base over all q in (0,q_max] is governed by the limit q downarrow 0. It equals the small-q base 4, while for any fixed positive q the Chebyshev endpoint base is rho(q)=(1+sqrt(1-q))^2<4. A two-sided lower bound with the single cap-level base 4/q for every fixed q<=q_max is false: the Chebyshev schedule itself has an upper bound with strictly smaller pointwise base rho(q)/q. The honest theorem is the pointwise two-sided bound.

directive: FRAMING / PROSE ONLY — the math is settled and MAXIMIZED (codex maximality review confirmed: matched two-sided minimax over the TV-envelope class P_beta with proved pointwise base rho(q)/q=(1+sqrt(1-q))^2/q; all theorem/lemma/def STATEMENTS and PROOFS are correct and must NOT be altered, narrowed, weakened, or re-opened; reuse every carried proof). Make ONLY these presentation changes so the RENDERED note matches what is actually proved: (1) In the TL;DR and the Setup glossary, REMOVE/retire the stale uniform base object rho_Ch(c,q_max) (it was replaced in round 1 by the proved pointwise base rho(q)/q — see the round-1 correction to thm:chebyshev-minimax) and state the headline base as the pointwise rho(q)/q with small-q limit 4/q. (2) Recenter the TL;DR/justification narrative on the PROVED headline: the TV-envelope Chebyshev-Lobatto matched-minimax over P_beta (bounded outcomes + round-mean variance envelope), NOT the Sobolev ellipsoid. (3) Demote the Sobolev-ellipsoid (ass:sobolev-ellipsoid) material so it is presented ONLY as the model subclass behind the exact-covariance OEQ (oeq:exact-nested-minimax), making explicit that the Sobolev structure is not load-bearing for the headline theorem. (4) Do not introduce any new assumption, object, or claim. Net effect: the headline the D0.5 reviewer sees is the proved TV-envelope matched-minimax with base rho(q)/q, with Sobolev confined to the open exact-covariance question.
note: D0 MAXIMALITY iterate (round 2) — framing-only recenter per codex maximality adjudication; no math change.

directive: D0.5 NON-CONVERGING source fixes — apply FIVE narrow, faithful edits (codex-adjudicated all_faithful=true, zero laundering, no finding is a real math defect; reuse ALL existing proofs/constants except the one added inline derivation in fix 3; NO weakening, NO new assumptions). FIX 1 (redundant_dependency@prop:equal-spacing-benchmark): DROP the dependency edge prop:equal-spacing-benchmark -> thm:tv-envelope-design and remove 'Under thm:tv-envelope-design' from the statement; restate as 'For each q_max in (0,1) there exists C_eq(q_max)>0 such that A_beta(p^eq(beta,q)) <= C_eq(q_max)(beta/q)^(2beta) for every beta in N and q in (0,q_max].' It is a pure Lagrange/A_beta bound; thm:tv-envelope-design stays only as the separate variance bridge. FIX 2 (redundant_dependency@thm:chebyshev-minimax): DROP the dependency edge thm:chebyshev-minimax -> thm:tv-envelope-design. Prerequisites are ONLY def:budgeted-schedule-class, def:amplification-criterion, def:chebyshev-schedule, lem:amplification-dual-norm, lem:continuous-chebyshev-endpoint-bound, lem:oversampled-chebyshev-lobatto-norming, lem:chebyshev-schedule-admissible. Add one sentence: 'This theorem is an approximation-theoretic minimax statement about A_beta and M_{beta,k,q}; thm:tv-envelope-design is used separately to translate this amplification minimax into the rollout TV-envelope variance statement.' No law-class assumptions added. FIX 3 (standard_result_not_discharged@lem:continuous-chebyshev-endpoint-bound): DISCHARGE the Chebyshev extremal inequality inline (do not merely cite). Replace the proof's first sentence with the alternation argument: 'Fix x_0>1 and let P be a real polynomial of degree at most beta with sup_{[-1,1]}|P|<=1. After replacing P by -P it suffices to prove P(x_0)<=T_beta(x_0). Suppose P(x_0)>T_beta(x_0); set t_j=cos(j pi/beta), j=0..beta. Since T_beta(t_j)=(-1)^j and |P(t_j)|<=1, H=P-T_beta satisfies H(t_j)<=0 for even j and H(t_j)>=0 for odd j, giving (with multiplicity) at least beta zeros of H on [-1,1]. Also H(1)<=0 while H(x_0)>0, so H has a further zero in (1,x_0): at least beta+1 zeros with deg H<=beta, a contradiction. Hence |P(x_0)|<=T_beta(x_0).' Then continue with the existing cosh/lambda calculation. Optional parenthetical cite: Rivlin, The Chebyshev Polynomials, Wiley 1974. FIX 4 (closest-math-comparator-gap@thm:chebyshev-minimax): ADD to Related work (and mirror in the theorem Gap sentence) the nearest-math comparator + non-subsumption: 'The closest mathematical comparators are classical polynomial extrapolation and optimal recovery: the Chebyshev/Remez endpoint-growth extremal problem, Schoenberg's approximation-theory line, and the Micchelli-Rivlin optimal-recovery program already study how Chebyshev geometry controls recovery of out-of-interval polynomial functionals. The theorem does NOT claim endpoint growth itself is new; its non-subsumed object is the finite-population rollout DESIGN minimax over S_{k,q} for the l1 amplification A_beta(p), with W_beta(p) enforcing causal TTE unbiasedness and thm:tv-envelope-design translating A_beta into the sharp TV-envelope design variance — ingredients absent from classical regression/optimal-recovery formulations.' Add bibitems Rivlin1974, MicchelliRivlin1977. FIX 5 (specialization-headline-drift@oeq:exact-nested-minimax): RECENTER the headline on the PROVED TV-envelope theorem. Retitle the section to 'exp_rollout_chebyshev_minimax (TV-envelope rollout design)'. Rewrite the TL;DR and Fill so the proved contribution is the linear-unbiased weights W_beta(p), the sharp TV-envelope variance (sigma_0^2/n)A_beta(p), the equal-spacing benchmark, the q=1 boundary, and the Chebyshev-Lobatto matched-minimax over S_{k,q} with pointwise base rho(q)/q, rho(q)=(1+sqrt(1-q))^2. REMOVE rho_Ch(c,q_max) and Sobolev from the headline/TL;DR/Fill (replace 'named base rho_Ch(c,q_max)' with 'pointwise base rho(q)/q'). Keep ass:sobolev-ellipsoid, P_{beta,s,L}, Gamma_P(p), R_exact, rho_Ch ONLY as exact-covariance/OEQ bookkeeping; if rho_Ch stays in the glossary, relabel it from named_minimax_base to OEQ-summary and note it is NOT the base used in thm:chebyshev-minimax.
note: D0.5 non-converging fix (round 3) — 5 faithful source fixes (dependency decouple x2, inline Chebyshev discharge, comparator+non-subsumption, headline recenter). Codex all_faithful=true, no math defect. Supersedes the round-2 framing directive.

changed.from: For every schedule p in S_{k,q} and every weight vector w in W_beta(p), the estimator hat_tau_{w,p} is design-unbiased for tau_P over P in P_beta, and Var_pi(hat_tau_{w,p}) <= sigma_0^2 n^(-1) (sum_{j=0}^k |w_j|)^2. Consequently the optimal schedule under the sharp total-variation variance envelope is the minimax program M_{beta,k,q} = inf_{p in S_{k,q}} A_beta(p).
changed.to: For every schedule p in S_{k,q} and every weight vector w in W_beta(p), the estimator hat_tau_{w,p} is design-unbiased for tau_P over P in P_beta, and Var_pi(hat_tau_{w,p}) <= sigma_0^2 n^(-1) (sum_{j=0}^k |w_j|)^2. Consequently, over the sharp total-variation variance envelope: the best achievable design variance for a fixed schedule p is sigma_0^2 n^(-1) A_beta(p); the minimax value over budgeted schedules is M_{beta,k,q} = inf_{p in S_{k,q}} A_beta(p); and an optimal schedule is any minimizer p^* in S_{k,q} attaining A_beta(p^*) = M_{beta,k,q}.
changed.reason: optimizer_value_conflation: separate the optimal-VALUE M_{beta,k,q} (a scalar infimum) from an optimal-SCHEDULE (a minimizer in S_{k,q}); the prior final sentence equated a schedule with the scalar minimax program. Faithful clarification, no weakening; all content (unbiasedness, variance bound, minimax value) preserved.

changed.from: Let p^eq(beta,q) = (0, q / beta, 2 q / beta, ..., q). Under thm:tv-envelope-design and ass:low-budget-cap, there exists a positive constant C_eq(q_max) depending only on q_max such that A_beta(p^eq(beta,q)) <= C_eq(q_max) (beta / q)^(2 beta) for every beta in N and q in (0,q_max].
changed.to: Let p^eq(beta,q) = (0, q / beta, 2 q / beta, ..., q). Under ass:low-budget-cap, there exists a positive constant C_eq(q_max) depending only on q_max such that A_beta(p^eq(beta,q)) <= C_eq(q_max) (beta / q)^(2 beta) for every beta in N and q in (0,q_max].
changed.reason: redundant_dependency: the bound is a pure Lagrange/A_beta estimate and does not consume thm:tv-envelope-design; drop that redundant premise (keep ass:low-budget-cap for q<=q_max<1). Removing an unused hypothesis strengthens the proposition; no weakening.

note: D0.5 statement-level fixes: optimizer/value separation + drop redundant tv-envelope premise

directive: D0.5 in-place findings (statement-level findings already fixed in the proto: optimizer_value_conflation@thm:tv-envelope-design and redundant_dependency@prop:equal-spacing-benchmark — do NOT re-open those; reuse all proofs). Apply these FOUR in-place fixes, all faithful, NO weakening, NO new assumptions: (A) missing_existence_step@thm:chebyshev-minimax: add the existence step that the linear-unbiased weight set W_beta(p) is NONEMPTY for the schedules used. Add as a short proof step, not a new assumption. (B) standard_result_not_discharged@lem:continuous-chebyshev-endpoint-bound: DISCHARGE the Chebyshev extremal inequality inline. Replace the proof's first sentence with the alternation argument: 'Fix x_0>1 and let P be a real polynomial of degree at most beta with sup_{[-1,1]}|P|<=1. After replacing P by -P it suffices to prove P(x_0)<=T_beta(x_0). Suppose P(x_0)>T_beta(x_0); set t_j=cos(j pi/beta), j=0..beta. Since T_beta(t_j)=(-1)^j and |P(t_j)|<=1, H=P-T_beta satisfies H(t_j)<=0 for even j and H(t_j)>=0 for odd j, giving (with multiplicity) at least beta zeros of H on [-1,1]. Also H(1)<=0 while H(x_0)>0, so H has a further zero in (1,x_0): at least beta+1 zeros with deg H<=beta, a contradiction. Hence |P(x_0)|<=T_beta(x_0).' Then continue with the existing cosh/lambda calculation. Optional cite: Rivlin, The Chebyshev Polynomials, Wiley 1974. (C) closest-math-comparator-gap@thm:chebyshev-minimax: ADD to Related work (mirror in the theorem Gap sentence) the nearest-math comparator + non-subsumption: 'The closest mathematical comparators are classical polynomial extrapolation and optimal recovery — the Chebyshev/Remez endpoint-growth extremal problem, Schoenberg approximation theory, and the Micchelli-Rivlin optimal-recovery program already study how Chebyshev geometry controls recovery of out-of-interval polynomial functionals. The theorem does NOT claim endpoint growth is new; its non-subsumed object is the finite-population rollout DESIGN minimax over S_{k,q} for the l1 amplification A_beta(p), with W_beta(p) enforcing causal TTE unbiasedness and thm:tv-envelope-design translating A_beta into the sharp TV-envelope design variance — ingredients absent from classical regression/optimal-recovery formulations.' Add bibitems Rivlin1974 and MicchelliRivlin1977. (D) specialization-headline-drift@oeq:exact-nested-minimax: RECENTER the title/TL;DR/Fill/honest-scope on the PROVED TV-envelope Chebyshev matched-minimax over P_beta with pointwise base rho(q)/q=(1+sqrt(1-q))^2/q. Retitle to 'exp_rollout_chebyshev_minimax (TV-envelope rollout design)'. Remove rho_Ch(c,q_max) and Sobolev from the headline/TL;DR/Fill (replace 'named base rho_Ch(c,q_max)' with 'pointwise base rho(q)/q'). changed.from: For every schedule p in S_{k,q} and every weight vector w in W_beta(p), the estimator hat_tau_{w,p} is design-unbiased for tau_P over P in P_beta, and Var_pi(hat_tau_{w,p}) <= sigma_0^2 n^(-1) (sum_{j=0}^k |w_j|)^2. Consequently, over the sharp total-variation variance envelope: the best achievable design variance for a fixed schedule p is sigma_0^2 n^(-1) A_beta(p); the minimax value over budgeted schedules is M_{beta,k,q} = inf_{p in S_{k,q}} A_beta(p); and an optimal schedule is any minimizer p^* in S_{k,q} attaining A_beta(p^*) = M_{beta,k,q}.
changed.to: For every schedule p in S_{k,q} and every weight vector w in W_beta(p), the estimator hat_tau_{w,p} is design-unbiased for tau_P over P in P_beta, and Var_pi(hat_tau_{w,p}) <= sigma_0^2 n^(-1) (sum_{j=0}^k |w_j|)^2. Consequently, over the sharp total-variation variance envelope: the best achievable design variance for a fixed schedule p is sigma_0^2 n^(-1) A_beta(p); the dimensionless minimax amplification value over budgeted schedules is M_{beta,k,q} = inf_{p in S_{k,q}} A_beta(p); the corresponding minimax design variance over budgeted schedules is sigma_0^2 n^(-1) M_{beta,k,q}; and an optimal schedule for the variance envelope is any minimizer p^* in S_{k,q} attaining A_beta(p^*) = M_{beta,k,q}.
changed.reason: Fixes scale_drop by reserving M_{beta,k,q} for the dimensionless amplification and naming the schedule-level variance minimax as (sigma_0^2/n) M_{beta,k,q}.

note: D0.5 r3: scale_drop fix on tv-envelope-design (dimensionless M vs variance (s0^2/n)M)

directive: Apply only proof-body/comparator/bibliography edits in the D0.R in-place loop; the proto already fixes thm:tv-envelope-design scale, estimand_functional, TLDR, and Fill. For lem:continuous-chebyshev-endpoint-bound, replace the Chebyshev-extremal invocation with this proof. First, for beta=0 the claim is trivial after enlarging constants; assume beta>=1. Fix x0>1 and let P be real with deg(P)<=beta and sup_{[-1,1]} |P| <= 1. Since T_beta(x0)>0, suppose for contradiction that |P(x0)| > T_beta(x0). Choose s in {-1,1} so s P(x0) > T_beta(x0), set lambda = s P(x0)/T_beta(x0) > 1, and define H(x)=s P(x)-lambda T_beta(x). Let t_j=cos(j pi/beta), j=0,...,beta. Then T_beta(t_j)=(-1)^j. If j is even, H(t_j) <= 1-lambda < 0; if j is odd, H(t_j) >= -1+lambda > 0. The signs are strict, so for each j=0,...,beta-1 the intermediate value theorem gives a zero of H in the open interval between t_j and t_{j+1}. These beta zeros lie in (-1,1), and H(x0)=0 gives one further zero outside [-1,1]. Thus H has at least beta+1 distinct zeros although deg(H)<=beta, a contradiction. Hence |P(x0)|<=T_beta(x0). This is the classical Chebyshev extremal inequality; cite Rivlin1974, The Chebyshev Polynomials, Ch. 1/early extremal-property section. Now take x0=x_q. Writing x_q=cosh a and lambda(q)=exp(a), T_beta(x_q)=(lambda(q)^beta+lambda(q)^(-beta))/2, so |R(x_q)-R(-1)| <= T_beta(x_q)+1 <= C(q_max) lambda(q)^beta because lambda(q)>=lambda(q_max)>1. For the lower order, T_beta(-1)=(-1)^beta, so |T_beta(x_q)-T_beta(-1)| >= T_beta(x_q)-1 = (1/2)lambda(q)^beta(1-lambda(q)^(-beta))^2 >= (1/2)(1-lambda(q_max)^(-1))^2 lambda(q)^beta. This gives explicit positive constants C(q_max) and c(q_max). For lem:oversampled-chebyshev-lobatto-norming, keep the statement and replace the bandwidth selection paragraph with a finite-exception argument. Fix c>1 and set delta=(c-1)/(4c). Then gamma=1-delta-(1+delta)/c > 0. For all k large enough, the interval [(1+delta)beta,(1-delta)k] has length at least one whenever k>=c beta, so choose an integer m in it. Then beta<m<k, beta/m <= 1/(1+delta), and m/k <= 1-delta, with constants depending only on c. Use the de la Vallee Poussin trigonometric kernel V_m,beta whose multiplier is one on |ell|<=beta, tapers on beta<|ell|<m, and vanishes for |ell|>=m. For Q(theta)=R(-cos theta), discrete orthogonality on the 2k grid is exact because m<k, giving Q(theta)=(1/(2k)) sum_{r=0}^{2k-1} Q(pi r/k) V_m,beta(theta-pi r/k). The standard de la Vallee Poussin L1 bound, with the two separation ratios above, gives sup_theta (1/(2k)) sum_r |V_m,beta(theta-pi r/k)| <= K_large(c), hence ||Q||_infty <= K_large(c) max_r |Q(pi r/k)|. For the remaining pairs with k below the large-pair threshold, there are only finitely many (beta,k) satisfying k>=c beta; for each, the sampling map from polynomials of degree <= beta to values on the Lobatto grid is injective because k>=c beta>beta gives at least beta+1 distinct nodes, so its inverse norm is finite. Let K_fin(c) be the maximum of those finitely many inverse norms and set K(c)=max(K_large(c),K_fin(c)). Because Q is even, the 2k-grid maximum equals max_{0<=j<=k}|R(-cos(pi j/k))|. This discharges the k=beta+1-type cases without changing the lemma statement. For the comparator fix, replace the generic comparator paragraph with: The closest approximation-theory comparators are theorem-specific. Rivlin1974, in the Chebyshev extremal theorem for polynomials bounded on [-1,1], gives sup{|P(x0)|: deg(P)<=beta, ||P||_{infty,[-1,1]}<=1}=T_beta(x0) for fixed x0>1; this supplies the endpoint-growth inequality but has no rollout schedule, no budget class S_{k,q}, and no variance objective. Schoenberg-Whitney1953 proves the total-positivity/interpolation criterion for spline collocation matrices under the Schoenberg-Whitney interlacing conditions; it supplies a classical interpolation-stability template, not an exterior-point polynomial design minimax for causal rollout measurements. MicchelliRivlin1977 develops optimal recovery of linear functionals from prescribed information in smoothness classes; it optimizes recovery algorithms for a fixed information structure, not the treated-fraction measurement schedule under causal TTE unbiasedness. The new object in thm:chebyshev-minimax is the finite-population rollout design minimax over p in S_{k,q} for the l1 amplification A_beta(p), where W_beta(p) enforces tau_P-unbiased rollout weights, q is a hard treatment-budget endpoint, and thm:tv-envelope-design converts the dimensionless amplification into the sharp TV-envelope design variance (sigma_0^2/n)M_{beta,k,q}.
note: D0.5 r3 proof/prose directive (round 5): Chebyshev-endpoint rigorous discharge + Lobatto norming proof fix + specific-citation comparator. Statement(scale_drop) + fields(estimand_functional/tldr/fill/bib) already in proto.

changed.from: For every schedule p in S_{k,q} and every weight vector w in W_beta(p), the estimator hat_tau_{w,p} is design-unbiased for tau_P over P in P_beta, and Var_pi(hat_tau_{w,p}) <= sigma_0^2 n^(-1) (sum_{j=0}^k |w_j|)^2. Consequently, over the sharp total-variation variance envelope: the best achievable design variance for a fixed schedule p is sigma_0^2 n^(-1) A_beta(p); the dimensionless minimax amplification value over budgeted schedules is M_{beta,k,q} = inf_{p in S_{k,q}} A_beta(p); the corresponding minimax design variance over budgeted schedules is sigma_0^2 n^(-1) M_{beta,k,q}; and an optimal schedule for the variance envelope is any minimizer p^* in S_{k,q} attaining A_beta(p^*) = M_{beta,k,q}.
changed.to: Assume beta>=1 and k>=beta. For every schedule p in S_{k,q} and every weight vector w in W_beta(p), the estimator hat_tau_{w,p} is design-unbiased for tau_P over P in P_beta, and Var_pi(hat_tau_{w,p}) <= sigma_0^2 n^(-1) (sum_{j=0}^k |w_j|)^2. Consequently, since W_beta(p) is nonempty for every p in S_{k,q}, over the sharp total-variation variance envelope the best achievable design variance for a fixed schedule p is sigma_0^2 n^(-1) A_beta(p); the dimensionless minimax amplification value over budgeted schedules is M_{beta,k,q}=inf_{p in S_{k,q}} A_beta(p); the corresponding minimax design variance over budgeted schedules is sigma_0^2 n^(-1) M_{beta,k,q}; and an optimal schedule for the variance envelope is any minimizer p^* in S_{k,q} attaining A_beta(p^*)=M_{beta,k,q}.
changed.reason: feasibility_gap: add k>=beta so W_beta(p) nonempty (Vandermonde); A_beta/M well-defined. Does not weaken headline (k=ceil(c beta)>=beta).

note: D0.5 r4: feasibility_gap fix (k>=beta => W_beta nonempty)

directive:
D0.5 final proof fix (lem:oversampled-chebyshev-lobatto-norming). The statement (k>=c beta, uniform K(c)) is unchanged; REPLACE the proof entirely with the Ehlich-Zeller Chebyshev-Lobatto norming inequality (NO de la Vallee Poussin, NO small-beta branch). Replace the proof with the Chebyshev-Lobatto norming inequality of Ehlich-Zeller. A convenient sharp form is: if R is a real polynomial of degree at most beta and 0 <= beta < k, then

  sup_{x in [-1,1]} |R(x)| <= sec(pi beta/(2k)) max_{0<=j<=k} |R(-cos(pi j/k))|.

Equivalently, if beta/k <= theta < 1, then the norming constant is K(theta)=sec(pi theta/2). This is the Chebyshev-Lobatto/extrema-grid form of the Ehlich-Zeller mesh inequality; cite Ehlich and Zeller (1964), Schwankung von Polynomen zwischen Gitterpunkten, Math. Z. 86, 41--44. Hence under k>=c beta one has beta/k <= 1/c and may take

  K(c)=sec(pi/(2c)) = 1/cos(pi/(2c)).

Full proof text: Put Q(t)=R(-cos t). Then Q is a real even trigonometric polynomial of degree at most beta, and sup_{x in [-1,1]}|R(x)|=sup_{t in [0,pi]}|Q(t)|. Let S=max_{0<=j<=k}|Q(pi j/k)| and M=sup_{t in [0,pi]}|Q(t)|. If M=0 there is nothing to prove, so assume M>0. Choose t0 in [0,pi] with |Q(t0)|=M and, after replacing Q by -Q if necessary, assume Q(t0)=M. Choose j with |t0-pi j/k|<=pi/(2k). Apply the Bernstein-Ehlich-Zeller differential inequality for normalized real trigonometric polynomials of degree beta: for U=Q/M, |U'(t)| <= beta sqrt(1-U(t)^2) wherever |U(t)|<1. Equivalently, along the interval from t0 to pi j/k, arccos U can increase at speed at most beta. Since U(t0)=1, this gives arccos U(pi j/k) <= beta |t0-pi j/k| <= pi beta/(2k). Because beta<k, the right side is < pi/2, so U(pi j/k) >= cos(pi beta/(2k)). Therefore S >= |Q(pi j/k)| >= M cos(pi beta/(2k)), proving M <= sec(pi beta/(2k)) S. Finally k>=c beta implies beta/k<=1/c, so sec(pi beta/(2k))<=sec(pi/(2c)). Returning to R gives the claimed bound with K(c)=sec(pi/(2c)). This removes the de la Vallee Poussin bandwidth choice and has no small-beta branch. Also ensure the bibliography includes  ibitem{EhlichZeller1964} Ehlich, H., and Zeller, K. (1964). Schwankung von Polynomen zwischen Gitterpunkten. Mathematische Zeitschrift 86, 41-44. The feasibility_gap on thm:tv-envelope-design (k>=beta, W_beta nonempty) is already fixed in the proto statement; do not re-open it.
note: D0.5 r4 Lobatto proof: Ehlich-Zeller norming K(c)=sec(pi/2c), removes dVP bandwidth gap. Feasibility (k>=beta) already in proto.

changed.from: Let p^eq(beta,q) = (0, q / beta, 2 q / beta, ..., q). Under ass:low-budget-cap, there exists a positive constant C_eq(q_max) depending only on q_max such that A_beta(p^eq(beta,q)) <= C_eq(q_max) (beta / q)^(2 beta) for every beta in N and q in (0,q_max].
changed.to: Let p^eq(beta,q) = (0, q / beta, 2 q / beta, ..., q). There exists a universal positive constant C_eq (one may take C_eq = 9) such that A_beta(p^eq(beta,q)) <= C_eq (beta / q)^(2 beta) for every beta in N and every q in (0,1].
changed.reason: redundant_assumption: the Lagrange/A_beta bound uses only q>0 (endpoint x=1/q); the low-budget premise q<=q_max<1 does not feed the proof and narrows a bound that holds on all of (0,1]. Remove ass:low-budget-cap; C_eq is universal (=9). Strengthens the proposition.

note: D0.5 r5: equal-spacing universal C_eq on (0,1] (drop redundant cap)

directive: D0.5 r5 hygiene (both fixes already in proto: (i) thm:chebyshev-minimax depends_on now includes def:unbiased-weight-set; (ii) prop:equal-spacing-benchmark drops the redundant ass:low-budget-cap and states a universal C_eq=9 on q in (0,1]). Two proof notes: (A) In prop:equal-spacing-benchmark's proof, cover the full range q in (0,1]: for q in (0,1) use the existing Lagrange bound sum_j|L_j(1/q)|<=2(beta/q)^beta giving A_beta<=9(beta/q)^{2beta}; for q=1 the endpoint rule (w_0=-1,w_beta=1) gives A_beta(p^eq(beta,1))<=4<=9 beta^{2beta}=9(beta/1)^{2beta}. So C_eq=9 works uniformly on (0,1]. (B) In thm:chebyshev-minimax keep the explicit W_beta(p)-nonempty step citing def:unbiased-weight-set (k=ceil(c beta)>=beta+1 distinct nodes => Vandermonde-solvable moment system). Do NOT re-introduce any low-budget-cap dependence into equal-spacing.
note: D0.5 r5 dependency hygiene: def:unbiased-weight-set dep added; equal-spacing universal C_eq on (0,1].

changed.from: Under ass:low-budget-cap, does the shifted Chebyshev-Lobatto schedule also solve the exact finite-population nested-rollout minimax problem R_exact(beta,k,q), where Gamma_P(p) is the true covariance matrix of the monotone Bernoulli rollout rather than the total-variation variance envelope?
changed.to: Under ass:low-budget-cap, with beta>=1, k=ceil(c beta), q in (0,q_max], and the admissible Chebyshev schedule p^Ch(k,q) in S_{k,q} (lem:chebyshev-schedule-admissible), does the shifted Chebyshev-Lobatto schedule also solve the exact finite-population nested-rollout minimax problem R_exact(beta,k,q), where Gamma_P(p) is the true covariance matrix of the monotone Bernoulli rollout rather than the total-variation variance envelope? The Chebyshev schedule is rate-feasible for R_exact by lem:exact-risk-envelope-upper; exact optimality is open.
changed.reason: undeclared_support: make the admissibility support p^Ch in S_{k,q} (lem:chebyshev-schedule-admissible) and the rate-feasibility route (lem:exact-risk-envelope-upper) explicit in the OEQ.

note: D0.5 r6 ROOT REFRAME: reposition as TV-envelope rollout DESIGN theorem; classical optimal-recovery credited; Sobolev->OEQ only; poly-identity=inherited setup; oeq admissibility explicit

directive: D0.5 r6 ROOT REFRAME (codex-adjudicated: honest tier FIELD under the TV-envelope rollout-DESIGN framing; keep driving). All edits are in the proto (related_work, honest_scope, per-node gap/justification, oeq). When rendering: (1) Position the paper as a CAUSAL ROLLOUT TV-ENVELOPE OPTIMAL-DESIGN result whose mathematical engine is CLASSICAL Chebyshev/Ehlich-Zeller optimal recovery — NOT as a Sobolev-ellipsoid theorem and NOT as a rediscovery of the rollout polynomial identity. (2) Comparator: state theorem-by-theorem that the endpoint-extrapolation math is classical (Rivlin1974 Chebyshev extremal, EhlichZeller1964 norming; MicchelliRivlin1977 optimal recovery is the closest theorem-level comparator); the NON-SUBSUMED object is the finite-population rollout design minimax over S_{k,q} for W_beta(p)-unbiased TTE under the TV l1 amplification A_beta, plus the (sigma_0^2/n)M variance translation. (3) prop:rollout-polynomial-identity is INHERITED SETUP from CortezEichhornYu2022, not a contribution. (4) The sobolev_ellipsoid subclass is NOT the delivered contribution; it enters ONLY as the model subclass for the OPEN exact-covariance question (oeq). Keep all proofs; no math changes, no weakening.
note: D0.5 r6 root reframe: TV-envelope design framing, classical optimal-recovery credited, Sobolev->OEQ, poly-identity=inherited setup. Codex tier=field, keep_driving.

changed.from: Let p^eq(beta,q) = (0, q / beta, 2 q / beta, ..., q). There exists a universal positive constant C_eq (one may take C_eq = 9) such that A_beta(p^eq(beta,q)) <= C_eq (beta / q)^(2 beta) for every beta in N and every q in (0,1].
changed.to: Let p^eq(beta,q) = (0, q / beta, 2 q / beta, ..., q). There exists a universal positive constant C_eq (one may take C_eq = 9) such that A_beta(p^eq(beta,q)) <= C_eq (beta / q)^(2 beta) for every integer beta >= 1 and every q in (0,1].
changed.reason: scope_gap: the proof divides by beta, so the valid scope is beta>=1, not beta in N (which includes 0).

changed.from: Fix c > 1, q_max in (0,1), k=ceil(c beta), and define the pointwise Chebyshev base rho(q)=(1+sqrt(1-q))^2. There exist positive constants C_-(q_max) and C_+(c,q_max) such that, for every beta>=1 and q in (0,q_max], every p in S_{k,q} satisfies A_beta(p) >= C_-(q_max) (rho(q)/q)^(2 beta), while the shifted Chebyshev-Lobatto schedule satisfies A_beta(p^Ch(k,q)) <= C_+(c,q_max) (rho(q)/q)^(2 beta). Hence M_{beta,k,q} has pointwise low-budget exponential base rho(q)/q; as q downarrow 0 this is 4/q, the intrinsic exponent is 2 beta, and the equal-spacing beta/q factor is not minimax in beta once the rollout uses more than beta+1 nodes.
changed.to: Fix c > 1 and q_max in (0,1), and define the pointwise Chebyshev base rho(q)=(1+sqrt(1-q))^2. There exist positive constants C_-(q_max) and C_+(c,q_max) such that, for every beta>=1, every integer k >= c beta, and every q in (0,q_max], every schedule p in S_{k,q} satisfies A_beta(p) >= C_-(q_max) (rho(q)/q)^(2 beta), while the shifted Chebyshev-Lobatto schedule satisfies A_beta(p^Ch(k,q)) <= C_+(c,q_max) (rho(q)/q)^(2 beta). Hence M_{beta,k,q} has pointwise low-budget exponential base rho(q)/q; as q downarrow 0 this is 4/q, the intrinsic exponent is 2 beta, and the equal-spacing beta/q factor is not minimax in beta once the rollout uses more than beta+1 nodes.
changed.reason: redundant_assumption: both proof branches use only k>=c beta (the oversampling inequality), not the stricter k=ceil(c beta); stating the theorem for every integer k>=c beta matches (and generalizes to) what the proof supports.

note: D0.5 r7: strip Sobolev (reposition to P_beta), scope beta>=1, generalize k>=c beta, precise theorem-level comparator

directive: D0.5 r7: (1) SOBOLEV FULLY STRIPPED — the paper is now entirely over the TV-envelope class P_beta; ass:sobolev-ellipsoid, def:sobolev-rollout-law-class, and symbols s,L,P_{beta,s,L} are removed; R_exact and the exact-covariance OEQ are defined over P_beta (the exact-risk bridge used only the variance envelope, never Sobolev). Do NOT reintroduce any Sobolev object; render no sobolev_ellipsoid claim. (2) SCOPE: prop:equal-spacing-benchmark now beta>=1; thm:chebyshev-minimax now 'every integer k>=c beta' (generalized from k=ceil(c beta)). (3) COMPARATOR: the thm:chebyshev-minimax gap now carries a precise theorem-level non-subsumption vs Rivlin1974 (Chebyshev extremal), EhlichZeller1964 (mesh norming), MicchelliRivlin1977 (optimal recovery) — render it: none optimizes the sampling SET under a budget nor targets a causal estimand; the non-subsumed object is the budgeted rollout DESIGN minimax over S_{k,q} for the TTE-unbiased l1 amplification. Keep all proofs; no math weakening.
note: D0.5 r7: Sobolev strip to P_beta + scope(beta>=1,k>=c beta) + precise theorem-level comparator.

changed.from: Under ass:low-budget-cap, with beta>=1, k=ceil(c beta), q in (0,q_max], and the admissible Chebyshev schedule p^Ch(k,q) in S_{k,q} (lem:chebyshev-schedule-admissible), does the shifted Chebyshev-Lobatto schedule also solve the exact finite-population nested-rollout minimax problem R_exact(beta,k,q), where Gamma_P(p) is the true covariance matrix of the monotone Bernoulli rollout rather than the total-variation variance envelope? The Chebyshev schedule is rate-feasible for R_exact by lem:exact-risk-envelope-upper; exact optimality is open.
changed.to: Under ass:low-budget-cap, with beta>=1, k=ceil(c beta), q in (0,q_max], and the admissible Chebyshev schedule p^Ch(k,q) in S_{k,q} (lem:chebyshev-schedule-admissible), does the shifted Chebyshev-Lobatto schedule also solve the exact finite-population nested-rollout minimax problem R_exact(beta,k,q), where Gamma_P(p) is the true covariance matrix of the monotone Bernoulli rollout rather than the total-variation variance envelope? The Chebyshev schedule is rate-feasible for R_exact by thm:chebyshev-minimax (the Chebyshev-schedule amplification upper bound) together with lem:exact-risk-envelope-upper (the envelope-to-exact-risk bridge); exact optimality is open.
changed.reason: wrong_support: rate-feasibility needs BOTH the Chebyshev-schedule upper bound (thm:chebyshev-minimax) and the envelope bridge (lem:exact-risk-envelope-upper), not the bridge alone.

directive: D0.5 r8: (1) RUN RELABELED — the specialization is now tv_envelope_rollout_design (the delivered field-tier P_beta TV-envelope rollout-DESIGN theorem); the Sobolev-ellipsoid label was non-load-bearing metadata and is retired. Position the flagship as the delivered P_beta theorem; render NO Sobolev/ellipsoid claim. (2) lem:exact-risk-envelope-upper (redundant_class_restriction): phrase the envelope-to-exact-risk bound as consuming ONLY ass:round-mean-variance-envelope — 'for every law satisfying the round-mean variance envelope Var<=sigma_0^2/n (in particular every P in P_beta, hence every law in the sup defining R_exact), w' Gamma_P(p) w = Var_pi(hat_tau_{w,p}) <= (sigma_0^2/n)(sum_j|w_j|)^2' — so the P_beta static-rollout/beta-polynomial restrictions are only ambient context of R_exact's domain, not consumed by the bound. (3) equal-spacing depends_on now includes def:unbiased-weight-set + def:amplification-criterion; oeq rate-feasibility support corrected to thm:chebyshev-minimax + lem:exact-risk-envelope-upper. Keep all proofs; no math weakening.
note: D0.5 r8: relabel to tv_envelope_rollout_design + lem envelope-only phrasing + equal-spacing/oeq deps. Codex: field, no remaining obstacle.
